vulnerability.reason: Both describe sensitivity to hidden misalignment from unrecoverable reward functions: reference says lack of interpretability masks misalignment until deployment, generated says reliance without identifiability prevents verification, thus hiding misalignment
vulnerability.reference: Lack of interpretability of inferred rewards masks misalignment until after deployment.
vulnerability.generated: Reliance on inverse reward inference for LLM alignment, without unique identifiability guarantees, prevents verification and correction of misspecified rewards.

impact.reason: Both describe affected party (users) and damage type (harmful advice/responses); generated adds trust erosion but core harm matches
impact.reference: Widespread dissemination of biased or harmful advice from conversational AI.
impact.generated: Misaligned language model outputs become brittle, generating harmful or untrustworthy responses that erode user trust and scale safety risks.

dose_response.reason: The causal chain from misspecified rewards to harmful outputs is present, but the reference includes deployment to conversational AI and disproportionate harm to marginalized groups, which are omitted.
dose_response.reference: Non-identifiable, misspecified reward functions recovered via IRL cause misaligned LLM outputs, which when deployed to conversational AI systems expose users to harmful advice, eroding trust and disproportionately harming marginalized groups.
dose_response.generated: Non-identifiable or misspecified recovered reward functions, through reliance by LLM developers and end-users on inverse reward inference, prevent verification and correction of misspecified rewards, leading to brittle, harmful language model outputs eroding user trust and scaling safety risks.
